fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Kansas Physical Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a physical therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Kansas - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: physical therapist license reciprocity kansas, how to transfer physical therapist license to kansas, physical therapist compact kansas, kansas physical therapist endorsement application

board.name: Kansas Board of Healing Arts — PT
board.parent_dept: Kansas Department for Aging and Disability Services (KDADS)
board.mailing_address: 800 SW Jackson, Lower Level – Suite A, Topeka, KS 66612

license_types.title: PT, DPT
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a physical therapist in Kansas.

steps.title: Create an Account and Start Application
steps.description: Access the Kansas Board of Healing Arts Online Application System at www.kansas.gov/kbha-online, select 'New User,' and follow the prompts to create an account and initiate your Physical Therapist license application.

steps.title: Complete Online Application and Pay Fee
steps.description: Fill out all required sections of the online application within the KBHA Online Application System and submit the non-refundable application fee. The application for endorsement follows the same process as an initial application.

steps.title: Submit Supporting Documentation
steps.description: Arrange for official transcripts from your physical therapy program and verification of any other state licenses to be sent directly from the source to the Kansas Board of Healing Arts. The applicant is responsible for ensuring all required documents are received.

steps.title: Complete Criminal Background Check
steps.description: Undergo a Kansas criminal background check, if required, by following the instructions provided by the Board. This typically involves fingerprinting.

steps.title: Pass Jurisprudence Exam & NPTE
steps.description: Successfully complete the Kansas Physical Therapy Jurisprudence Exam online and ensure your National Physical Therapy Examination (NPTE) scores are sent directly to the Board from FSBPT.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination).

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NPTE. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Physical Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Kansas physical therapist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination) is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Kansas have reciprocity for physical therapists?
faqs.answer: Yes. Kansas participates in the PT Compact. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in Kansas without a separate endorsement application.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my physical therapist license to Kansas?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Kansas while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Physical Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Physical Therapist in Kansas
hero.dek: To apply for a Physical Therapist license in Kansas, create an account and complete the application through the Kansas Board of Healing Arts Online Application System.
